questions.question: Which ancient Indian university was renowned for its library and teachings during the Gupta period?
questions.explanation: Nalanda was one of the world's first residential universities and was famous during the Gupta era for its extensive library and scholarly activities.

questions.question: Who founded the Mughal Empire in India?
questions.explanation: Babur, a descendant of Timur and Genghis Khan, founded the Mughal Empire in 1526 after the Battle of Panipat.

questions.question: The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched in India in which year?
questions.explanation: The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1920 as a protest against British policies.

questions.question: Who was the leader of the Salt Satyagraha in 1930?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi led the Salt Satyagraha, also known as Dandi March, in 1930 to protest against salt taxes.

questions.question: Which river is known as the 'Sorrow of Bengal' due to frequent flooding?
questions.explanation: The Brahmaputra River causes extensive flooding in Assam and Bangladesh, earning it the name 'Sorrow of Bengal'.

questions.question: Which of the following is primarily responsible for the monsoon winds in India?
questions.explanation: The Southwest Monsoon winds, originating from the Indian Ocean, bring heavy rainfall to India from June to September.

questions.question: Which Indian state is the largest producer of Coal?
questions.explanation: Odisha is the largest producer of coal in India, contributing significantly to the country's coal output.

questions.question: Which is the longest river in India?
questions.explanation: The Godavari River, about 1,465 km long, is the longest river flowing entirely within India.

questions.question: Who is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha?
questions.explanation: The Vice President of India is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha as per the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: The concept of 'Judicial Review' in India was established by which Supreme Court case?
options: Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala
Minerva Mills Ltd. v. Union of India
S.P. Gupta v. Union of India
questions.explanation: In Minerva Mills Ltd. v. Union of India (1980), the Supreme Court reaffirmed the power of Judicial Review.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanation: Articles 14 to 18 of the Indian Constitution deal with the Fundamental Rights regarding equality and freedom.

questions.question: Who appoints the Chief Election Commissioner of India?
questions.explanation: The Chief Election Commissioner is appointed by the President of India.

questions.question: What is the main objective of the Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management (FRBM) Act, 2003?
questions.explanation: FRBM Act aims to reduce fiscal deficit and ensure fiscal discipline among the government.

questions.question: Which organization publishes the 'Financial Stability Report' in India?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) publishes the 'Financial Stability Report' biannually.

questions.question: India's GDP is primarily contributed by which sector?
questions.explanation: The services sector contributes the largest share to India’s GDP, followed by industry and agriculture.

questions.question: The 'Make in India' initiative was launched in which year?
questions.explanation: 'Make in India' was launched by the Government of India in 2014 to promote manufacturing.

questions.question: Which law explains the phenomenon of electromagnetic induction?
questions.explanation: Faraday's Law of Electromagnetic Induction states that a changing magnetic flux induces an electromotive force.

questions.question: Which part of the human cell is responsible for energy production?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are known as the powerhouse of the cell because they produce energy (ATP).

questions.question: The principle of laser is based on which phenomena?
questions.explanation: Laser operates on the principle of stimulated emission of photons.

questions.question: Which planet is known as the 'Red Planet'?
questions.explanation: Mars is called the 'Red Planet' due to its reddish appearance caused by iron oxide on its surface.

questions.question: Which national park is famous for Bengal tiger conservation in India?
questions.explanation: Sundarbans National Park is renowned for its Bengal tiger population and mangrove ecosystem.

questions.question: Which treaty aims to protect biodiversity and combat illegal trade in wildlife?
questions.explanation: CITES (Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species) regulates and prohibits illegal wildlife trade.

questions.question: Which of the following is a non-renewable resource?
questions.explanation: Coal is a non-renewable resource formed over millions of years and limited in quantity.

questions.question: The Kyoto Protocol is associated with which environmental issue?
questions.explanation: The Kyoto Protocol addresses global warming by reducing greenhouse gas emissions.

questions.question: Who is the current President of India as of June 2026?
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu has been serving as the President of India since 25 July 2022.

questions.question: Who is the current Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Narendra Modi has been the Prime Minister of India since 26 May 2014.

questions.question: Mohan Charan Majhi is the current Chief Minister of which Indian state?
questions.explanation: Mohan Charan Majhi is the current Chief Minister of Odisha since 12 June 2024.

questions.question: Who is the current External Affairs Minister of India?
questions.explanation: S. Jaishankar is the current External Affairs Minister of India since 2019.

questions.question: Who is the current Chief Justice of India (judge) as of June 2026?
questions.explanation: Surya Kant is serving as the Chief Justice of India since 24 November 2025.

questions.question: Who is the current Vice President of India?
questions.explanation: C. P. Radhakrishnan has been serving as the Vice President since 12 September 2025.
